frameworks.name: production-style responsibility attribution
frameworks.one_line: trait responsibility scales with a trait's predictive contribution to behavior

frameworks.name: counterfactual-style responsibility attribution
frameworks.one_line: responsibility judged by whether the outcome would change absent the trait

frameworks.name: folk-psychological model of personality traits as agents
frameworks.one_line: intra-personal trait society modeled like inter-personal group responsibility

frameworks.name: models of personal responsibility in groups
frameworks.one_line: prior group-responsibility formalism adapted to traits within one person

core_question: Do people attribute responsibility to personality traits the way they attribute responsibility to people in groups?
key_assumptions: traits can be reasoned about as quasi-agents inside a person
predictive relationships between traits and actions can be elicited and quantified
group-responsibility models transfer to intra-personal trait responsibility
manipulations: trait-action pairs whose predictive strength was varied
Experiment 1 elicited predictive relationships; Experiment 2 elicited responsibility judgments
alternative responsibility model classes (production-style vs counterfactual-style)
key_findings: responsibility judgments were best explained by a production-style model
responsibility scaled with a trait's predictive contribution to behavior
counterfactual-style models did not fit trait responsibility as well
trait responsibility shows both similarities to and differences from group responsibility
interpretations: folk psychology treats traits like members of an internal group, but weighs them by predictive contribution rather than counterfactual difference-making
trait responsibility attribution diverges from personal responsibility in groups specifically on counterfactual reasoning
